[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: How many people showed up to the Raptors' championship parade? (Hint: We don't know)
[METADATA/INTRO]: Today was a historic day for Toronto, for Canada, and for the game of basketball in this country. The Toronto Raptors are world champions for the first time since their founding in 1995. Soak it in. Here is a photo...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: He had some rules of thumb for that. A light crowd was about 1 person per 10 square feet. And a dense crowd (such as a mosh pit or an NBA championship parade in Toronto) was about 1 person per 2.5 square feet. 
 Using this method and aerial photos of today's parade, I would imagine that we could eventually get to a more precise estimate than 1 to 2 million people. But surely somebody has figured out how to program a drone (or other UAV) and do this even more accurately. 
 Crowd data is valuable information, particularly for political rallies and protests (I would imagine). If you know of a company doing this, please leave it in the comment section below. And if it doesn't yet exist, well then, now you have a new business idea.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2019-06-17
[TITLE]: Toronto Raptors celebration parade marred by violence, transit shutdowns - The Globe and Mail
[METADATA/INTRO]: Enormous crowds filled downtown Toronto on Monday to cheer on their team as the city celebrated its first NBA Championship, and first major championship in more than 20 years – but the festivities were marred by several isolated incidents. Hundreds...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Enormous crowds of Raptors fans packed a route through Toronto’s downtown to see the NBA champions first-hand. Hours later than scheduled, the team arrived at Nathan Phillips Square where they thanked fans for the outpouring of support. 
 But authorities had estimated Sunday that close to two million people would show up to watch the parade. 
 Stephen Doig, a professor at Arizona State University and an expert on crowd-size estimation, looked at aerial photos of the crowds and estimated the number was likely 600,000 at the most. 
 “You are safe to say ‘hundreds of thousands’ came out to see the parade. That’s a fabulous crowd, and a great credit to the Raptors and their fans. But saying 'two million’ is, frankly, ridiculous," he said.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018-10-24
[TITLE]: Trump's Texas MAGA Rally Has Familiar Cavalier Look | Cleveland, OH Patch
[METADATA/INTRO]: Politics & Government Trump’s Texas MAGA Rally Has Familiar Cavalier Look...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Houston's Police Chief Art Acevedo Tweeted out the actual, estimated attendance figures for the Monday Trump rally. He said there were between 18,000 and 19,000 people inside, and another 3,000 people outside the arena. He thanked everyone in attendance, including the protesters, for their peaceful behavior. 
 Find out what's happening in Clevelandfor free with the latest updates from Patch. 
 This is actually the second time this exact photograph has been used, incorrectly, as a sign of rabid political support. Radio host Laura Ingraham said the picture was captured outside a Trump rally in Phoenix, Arizona in 2017. 
 The picture was actually taken via drone shot by the Chronicle-Telegram's Bruce Bishop.
